title: Anti-poaching drone-dock placement: 2.4 GHz C2 voxel coverage at patrol altitudes
summary: Two candidate dock sites are being evaluated for an autonomous drone deployed against rhino-poaching incursions. The drone's mission envelope is 60 to 150 m AGL across a 12 km × 8 km patrol AOI. Op E computes a 3D voxel of received power at the drone's antenna, derives a pass/fail surface against C2 sensitivity, and emits the maximum-range envelope per altitude. The scenario is `restricted_species` because both dock locations and patrol routes are operationally sensitive.
demonstrates: Appendix E restricted_species class with auto-classification
voxel slicing (§6.6) — implied for downstream consumers
notes: P.528 (air-to-ground) is preferred for this scenario by the auto-select strategy (§4.4) because the operation is Op E with link_type drone_c2; P.1812 also fits. `voxel_lossless: false` keeps the default 0.5 dB quantization (§6.1, voxel row). Slice queries against the resulting voxel can extract per-altitude geotiffs for review without re-running the pipeline. With `home_site_ref` set, the engine flags voxels where the return-to-home leg would not close as a separate margin; this is informational, not a separate output key.
